content.problemStatement:
"It doesn't matter how tough you are, you are not going to win on your own."

NNN players advance to the 2nd round, the game is revealed to be "HoneyComb".

The players have to construct an array AAA. Each and every player is given "Ppopgi" (Dalgona candy),
and the iiith player has to carve a number AiA_iAi‚Äã in their "Ppopgi".

The players will qualify if the array AAA satisfies the following rules:


The **sum of the squares** of all the numbers carved should be a **perfect square**. Formally, ‚àëi=1n(Ai)2\sum\limits_{i=1}^{n}(A_i)^2i=1‚àën‚Äã(Ai‚Äã)2 = K2K^2K2 , where KKK is a natural number.
The number of distinct values in the array should not exceed min‚Å°(10,N)\min(10,N)min(10,N).
Gi-Hun wants everyone to qualify, so he asks you for help. Help him find an array AAA which satisfies these conditions.

**If there are multiple arrays that satisfy the conditions, you may find any of them.**

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Input Format

The first line of inputs contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the length of the array to be constructed.
Output Format
For each test case, print the array AAA you construct in the following format:

The first line should contain a single integer KKK, the number of distinct values in the array AAA
(1‚â§K‚â§min‚Å°(10,N)1 \leq K \leq \min(10, N)1‚â§K‚â§min(10,N)).
The next KKK lines hold the description of the array. The ithi^{\text{th}}ith of these lines should contain two integers xix_ixi‚Äã and yiy_iyi‚Äã, denoting that xix_ixi‚Äã occurs exactly yiy_iyi‚Äã times in AAA.

The pairs (xi,yi)(x_i, y_i)(xi‚Äã,yi‚Äã) should satisfy the following conditions:

1‚â§xi‚â§1091 \leq x_i \leq 10^91‚â§xi‚Äã‚â§109 and yi‚â•1y_i \geq 1yi‚Äã‚â•1 for every 1‚â§i‚â§K1 \leq i \leq K1‚â§i‚â§K
The xix_ixi‚Äã must be pairwise distinct, i.e, xi‚âÝxjx_i \neq x_jxi‚ÄãÓÄÝ=xj‚Äã if i‚âÝji \neq jiÓÄÝ=j
The sum of yiy_iyi‚Äã must be NNN, i.e, ‚àëi=1Kyi=N\sum_{i=1}^K y_i = N‚àëi=1K‚Äãyi‚Äã=N

Explanation:
Test Case 2: 222 occurs 222 times, 333 occurs once, and 444 occurs 222 times in AAA. The sum of squares is thus 22+22+32+42+42=492^2 + 2^2 + 3^2 + 4^2 + 4^2 = 4922+22+32+42+42=49, which is 727^272.
Test Case 3: 222 occurs twice, 333 occurs once, 444 occurs twice, and 242424 occurs once in AAA. The sum of squares is 22+22+32+42+42+242=6252^2 + 2^2 + 3^2 + 4^2 + 4^2 + 24^2 = 62522+22+32+42+42+242=625, which is 25225^2252.
